commonIssues: Not self-fertile: grow at least two plants for cross-pollination or fruit will not set (UMN, Almanac).
Plants reach 3-4 ft and sprawl; cage, stake or trellis to keep fruit off the soil.
Wet foliage and soil splash spread disease; water at the base, not overhead.
Reduce watering frequency once fruit has set to improve fruit quality.
Flowers abort above ~32C (90F) or below ~13C (55F); protect from heat and cold snaps.
Susceptible to early/late blight, anthracnose, powdery mildew, tobacco mosaic virus; rotate away from other nightshades (tomato, pepper, potato) for ~3 years.
Pests: cutworms, tomato hornworms, Colorado potato beetles.
Container growing needs a pot at least 30 cm (12 in) deep and more frequent watering than in-ground plants.

source.citation: University of Minnesota Extension — Growing tomatillos and ground cherries in home gardens (6-8 hrs sun; transplant after frost; water at base, even moisture; not self-fertile, grow more than one plant; cage/trellis support; balanced nitrogen, excess N delays fruiting; pests early/late blight, anthracnose, TMV, cutworms, hornworms, Colorado potato beetle; 12-in deep containers).

The Old Farmer's Almanac — Tomatillos: How to Plant, Grow, and Care for Tomatillo (spacing ~3 ft; ~1 inch rain/water per week, water once or twice a week; withstands drought and heat; not self-fertile, grow more than one plant; trellis/cage support; flowers abort above 90F/below 55F).

UC Master Gardener Program of Alameda County — Guide to Growing Tomatillos (at least 6 hours sun/day; plants grow up to 4 ft, need cage or stake support; reduce irrigation after fruit set; plant at least two of same variety for pollination; balanced fertilizer; susceptible to powdery mildew and nightshade diseases).

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder — Physalis ixocarpa (full sun; medium water, water regularly; 2.5-3.5 ft tall by 3-4 ft wide; USDA zones 8-10 as annual; sow after frost when night temps >=55F; subject to Solanaceae pests/diseases, avoid same site for 3 years).

Confidence note — feeding cadence (baseIntervalDays 10, fruitingIntervalDays 14, NPK) is conservative: sources specify a balanced fertilizer with low-to-moderate nitrogen and warn excess N delays fruit, but give no exact day interval. Container idealMaxLiters (38 L) is an estimate scaled to a 3-4 ft bushy plant; sources state a 12-in-deep pot minimum but no upper volume. hotWeatherMultiplier and rainSkipThresholdMm derived from the ~1 inch/week + heat-tolerance guidance, not a published per-day figure. Planting windows by zone generalized from frost-date guidance.
